key_insight: PF-DIC merges phase-field fracture modeling with digital image correlation to measure displacement, strain, and crack damage in a single full-field framework.
engineering_relevance: It improves crack-tip displacement accuracy and removes the need for manual crack masks, making DIC more robust for complex fracture monitoring and damage diagnostics.
method: Use a phase-field damage variable to represent complex crack morphologies in speckle-image correlation.
Jointly solve for displacement, strain, and damage fields within a finite-element DIC formulation.
Adapt the framework to selectively identify critical cracks under different loading or damage mechanisms.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports numerical examples showing PF-DIC can capture different crack types while improving displacement measurement accuracy near cracks.
watch_item: The method adds modeling and implementation complexity, so reliable calibration and finite-element setup will matter for practical use on experimental data.
paper_title: PF-DIC: Phase field digital image correlation for integrated full-field displacement, strain, and damage measurements
